_template_purpose: COPY this file to your project's 99-META/queries/ folder, rename it to <ProjectCode>-SEARCH-CONFIG.json, and fill in the blanks. The KEYWORD-FILTER.ps1 and PRE-FILTER.ps1 scripts in 00-SYSTEM-CORE/SEARCH-SYSTEM/ will read this file.
_instructions: 1. The ENGINE (KEYWORD-FILTER.ps1, PRE-FILTER.ps1, NOUN-PHRASE-INDEX.md template, SEARCH-STRATEGY.md) lives in 00-SYSTEM-CORE/SEARCH-SYSTEM/ and NEVER changes between projects.
2. The CONFIG (this file) lives in 01-PROJECTS/<ProjectCode>/99-META/queries/ and is UNIQUE per project.
3. To add a new project, copy this template, rename it, and fill in core_constructs, noun_phrases, context_anchors, and any other fields relevant to your project.

framework_layers._note: OPTIONAL. Only fill in if your PhD has named framework layers (L1, L2, L3, etc.). Otherwise delete this entire block.

noun_phrases._note: FLAT DICTIONARY of 2-3 word noun phrases that signal relevance. Each phrase gets a weight (1-10). The filter sums weights of all matched phrases.

must_not_have: <FILL: terms that signal WRONG context - auto-reject>

preferred: <FILL: who the study should be ABOUT>

journal_filter._note: OPTIONAL. Use this when running in Scopus/WoS to limit to high-quality journals.

lemmatize._note: Word-form collapse. Add project-specific variants here. Universal suffixes (developed->develop, etc.) are already in the engine defaults.
